@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M272/M273 engine

mainEntity.name: How much does an M272 or M273 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M272/M273 typically costs £2,500–£6,000 supply only, with fitting adding £800–£1,500 depending on labour rates [ESTIMATED]. V6 variants are generally cheaper than V8s; used examples can be found from £1,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the M272/M273 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M272/M273 rates 3.0★ out of 5 — smooth and durable when maintained, but pre-2008 M272 V6s suffer balance shaft sprocket wear that can be catastrophic if ignored. The M273 V8 is more robust overall.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M272/M273 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Balance shaft sprocket wear on the M272 (pre-2008) is the most notorious — a design flaw that causes timing chain rattle on cold start and eventual engine failure if untreated. Intake manifold flap failures and oil leaks are also common on both.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M272/M273?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M272 powers the C-Class W204, E-Class W211/W212, CLS W219, SLK R171/R172, and ML W164. The M273 powers the S-Class W221, CL C216, E-Class W212 V8s, ML/GL/G-Class V8s, and CLS W219 V8s — spanning 2005–2015.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M272/M273 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular oil changes and addressing the balance shaft issue early (if pre-2008), 150,000–200,000 miles is achievable. Many well-maintained examples exceed 200,000 miles; oil leaks and intake issues are the main wear items as mileage climbs.

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M272/M273 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M272 and M273 are smooth, high-revving naturally aspirated V6 and V8 petrols — generally robust, but balance shaft wear is their defining risk. Backed by real UK specialist data [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 75% confidence — based on UK specialist consensus and owner-forum reporting, pending proprietary enquiry data.

rows.score: 12 (Mixed: many repairs straightforward; balance shaft replacement and timing chain work are labour-intensive on V8s)
20 (Abundant — these were high-volume engines; parts widely available from Mercedes, aftermarket and breakers)
4 (Reconditioned cost as % of lowest typical host value — £4,250 on a £2,500–£3,500 car is often 120%+ → 4)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The M272/M273 are generally robust V6/V8s — but balance shaft sprocket wear (pre-2008) and intake manifold issues are common — buy with full history and listen for cold-start rattles."
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts wanting a smooth, powerful petrol Mercedes with a strong parts network and willing to address known issues pre‑emptively.
verdictRating.avoidIf: Budget buyers shopping sub-£3k examples; a major repair can easily exceed the car's value.

rows.variantBadge: V6 diesel derivative not included — petrol variants are rare

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M272/M273 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
